source.title: Computational design and engineering of self-assembling multivalent microproteins with therapeutic potential against SARS-CoV-2

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The paper addresses the need for broad-spectrum antivirals against evolving SARS-CoV-2 variants, which evade existing monovalent drugs. The goal is to computationally design self-assembling trivalent microproteins that simultaneously bind all three RBDs of the spike protein, enhancing avidity and neutralization breadth. The approach involves structure-guided linker design, MD simulation evaluation, experimental validation, and functional testing.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: Summarizes the four-step computational approach to engineer trivalent microproteins targeting SARS-CoV-2 spike RBDs. MiniACE2-based constructs were designed with different scaffolds and linkers; MP-5ff showed high trimerization efficiency, conformational homogeneity, and strong neutralization. The 5ff scaffold was then used to engineer Tr67, a trivalent nanobody with broad neutralization against Omicron variants. Cryo-EM confirmed Tr67 locks spike in 3-RBD-up conformation.
process_signals: Four-step approach: structure-guided linker design, molecular simulation evaluation, experimental validation, functional testing
MP-5ff identified as optimal construct
5ff scaffold generalized to nanobody Nb67 to create Tr67
Tr67 shows broad neutralization against Omicron variants including XBB.1 and XBB.1.5
Cryo-EM confirms designed binding mode
methods: Experimental validation (SEC, native-PAGE, BLI, pseudovirus neutralization)

section_gists.heading: Introduction
section_gists.gist: Motivation: SARS-CoV-2 mutations reduce efficacy of existing drugs and limit conserved epitopes. Multivalent drugs targeting trimeric spike could enhance potency and breadth. Existing multivalent approaches (tandem nanobodies, trimeric ACE2) have limitations: elongated structures reduce stability, large size/poor solubility of ACE2. Hypothesis: self-assembling trivalent microproteins with small, soluble binders and trimerization scaffolds can overcome these issues. Need for rational design methods.
process_signals: Problem: need for broad-spectrum antivirals against evolving variants
Limitations of monovalent drugs and existing multivalent formats
Hypothesis: multivalency enhances avidity and neutralization breadth
Goal: develop rational computational design approach
failures_or_limits: Tandem nanobodies may have reduced stability and increased degradation
Trimeric ACE2 has large size, poor solubility, steric hindrance, potential side effects

section_gists.heading: Structure-guided linker design
section_gists.gist: Selected miniACE2 as monovalent binder and two trimerization scaffolds: F-scaffold (T4 fibritin foldon) and C-scaffold (coiled-coil). Goal: trap 3-RBD-up conformation. Calculated minimum linker distances (19.37 Å for F, 17.98 Å for C). Used RosettaRemodel to sample linker conformations for (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n with n=2-5. n=2 too short; n=3,5 chosen (n=4 less preferable). Designed 8 constructs: MP-3ff, MP-5ff, MP-3rf, MP-5rf (F-scaffold with flexible/rigid linkers of length 3 or 5); MP-3fc, MP-5fc, MP-3rc, MP-5rc (C-scaffold).
process_signals: Selection of components: miniACE2, F-scaffold, C-scaffold
Design goal: trap 3-RBD-up conformation
Linker length determined by minimum distance and Rosetta sampling
n=2 linkers rejected (too short)
n=4 linkers rejected (irregular distance distribution)
n=3 and n=5 linkers selected for both flexible (GGGGS) and rigid (EAAAK) types
Eight constructs generated for evaluation
methods: Lagrange multiplier method for minimum distance
RosettaRemodel for linker conformational sampling (1000 trajectories per linker)
failures_or_limits: n=4 linkers had irregular distributions, not selected

section_gists.heading: Molecular simulation evaluation of trivalent constructs
section_gists.gist: Evaluated 8 constructs via MD simulations (3x300 ns each). Assessed self-assembly via MM/GBSA binding free energies between monomers. F-scaffold constructs generally had lower (more favorable) binding free energies than C-scaffold. MP-5ff had lowest relative binding free energy among F-scaffolds, indicating strongest self-assembly. MP-5rc best among C-scaffolds. Conformational homogeneity assessed via PCA-based free energy landscapes (FELs) and RMSD/Rg-based FELs. MP-5ff showed a single, narrow energy well, indicating high homogeneity. Other constructs showed multiple wells or broader distributions, suggesting conformational heterogeneity.
process_signals: MD simulations used to predict self-assembly and conformational homogeneity
MM/GBSA ranking: MP-5ff best among F-scaffolds, MP-5rc best among C-scaffolds
FEL analysis: MP-5ff has single narrow energy well (high homogeneity)
Other constructs show multiple wells or broad distributions (heterogeneity)
Computational predictions guide selection of MP-5ff as optimal candidate
methods: All-atom MD simulations (GROMACS, AMBER99SB-ILDN, 300 ns, triplicates)
PCA and free energy landscape construction (PC1/PC2, RMSD/Rg)
metrics: Free energy landscapes (number and shape of energy wells)
failures_or_limits: MP-5rf had higher relative binding free energy (less stable)
Many constructs showed multiple conformational states in FELs

section_gists.heading: Experimental validation and functional test
section_gists.gist: Expressed and purified 8 constructs in E. coli. SEC: F-scaffold proteins had sharper trimer peaks than C-scaffold; MP-5ff sharpest, MP-5rf two peaks. C-scaffold: only MP-5rc single trimer peak; others had monomer/dimer peaks. Native-PAGE: only MP-5ff single band (homogeneous); others multiple bands. BLI: MP-5ff KD <1 pM vs miniACE2 1.03 nM (1000-fold improvement). Pseudovirus neutralization: MP-5ff IC50 29 pM vs miniACE2 682 pM (23-fold improvement). Confirms computational predictions.
process_signals: SEC confirms trimerization efficiency: MP-5ff best, MP-5rf poor
Native-PAGE confirms conformational homogeneity: only MP-5ff single band
BLI shows 1000-fold affinity enhancement for MP-5ff over monovalent
Neutralization shows 23-fold improvement for MP-5ff
Experimental results align with computational rankings
methods: E. coli expression and Ni-NTA purification
failures_or_limits: MP-5rf showed two SEC peaks, multiple Native-PAGE bands (heterogeneous)
C-scaffold constructs mostly had lower trimer ratios and multiple conformations

section_gists.heading: Engineering of a broad-spectrum trivalent nanobody
section_gists.gist: Applied 5ff trimerization unit to nanobody Nb67 to create Tr67. MD simulations and FELs predicted Tr67 has single narrow energy well (homogeneous). SEC and Native-PAGE confirmed high trimerization efficiency and homogeneity. BLI: Tr67 KD <1 pM for Omicron BA.1 RBD, vs Nb67 KD 4.2 nM. Pseudovirus neutralization: Tr67 potently neutralized Omicron variants BA.1, BA.2, BA.2.75, BA.2.12.1, BA.3, BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, XBB.1.5; Nb67 only neutralized cluster 1 variants (BA.1, BA.2, BA.2.75, BA.2.12.1, BA.3). Tr67 showed broad-spectrum activity.
process_signals: Generalization of 5ff scaffold to nanobody Nb67
Computational evaluation of Tr67 predicted homogeneity
Experimental validation confirmed trimerization and homogeneity
Tr67 shows broad neutralization against Omicron variants, including XBB.1.5
Monovalent Nb67 fails against cluster 2 variants (BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, XBB.1.5)
methods: Fusion of Nb67 to 5ff via (GGGGS)5 linker
Pseudovirus neutralization assay against multiple Omicron variants
failures_or_limits: Monovalent Nb67 loses neutralization against cluster 2 variants due to mutations at binding interface

section_gists.heading: Cryo-EM analysis of Tr67-spike complex
section_gists.gist: Cryo-EM structure of Tr67 with Omicron BA.1 spike at 9 Å resolution. Confirmed Tr67 binds all three RBDs in 3-RBD-up conformation, locking them in inactive state. MD flexible fitting validated designed binding mode. Interface analysis: Tr67 has larger contact area and more interactions than monovalent Nb67. Docking simulations explained variant specificity: Nb67 fails to dock to cluster 2 variants at expected epitope due to mutations.
process_signals: Cryo-EM confirms designed binding mode: Tr67 binds all three RBDs
Tr67 induces 3-RBD-up conformation, rare for Omicron
Tr67 locks RBDs, blocking ACE2 binding and potentially facilitating immune clearance
Interface analysis shows enhanced interactions for Tr67
Docking explains why Nb67 fails against cluster 2 variants
failures_or_limits: Monovalent Nb67 docking to cluster 2 variants fails at expected epitope

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Summarizes success of computational approach in designing MP-5ff and Tr67. Highlights precise geometric matching, reduced screening space, and rapid development. Cryo-EM reveals novel 3-RBD-up induction mechanism by trivalent binding. Multivalency provides avidity, broader interfaces, and resistance to escape. Tr67 suitable for large-scale E. coli production. Limitations: in vivo efficacy, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, biosafety not evaluated. Future work needed.
process_signals: Approach enables rational design, reducing trial-and-error
Tr67 induces 3-RBD-up via synergistic binding, distinct from antibody combinations
Multivalency enhances avidity and breadth
Limitations acknowledged: in vivo studies needed
failures_or_limits: In vivo therapeutic efficacy not tested

section_gists.heading: Structure-guided design of linkers
section_gists.gist: RosettaRemodel used for linker design. Blueprint file specifies connectivity, secondary structure, sequence. Fragment insertion with Ramachandran restraints. Tested (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n for n=2-5. 1000 independent trajectories per linker, lowest-energy models saved. UCSF ChimeraX for visualization.
process_signals: RosettaRemodel with fragment insertion for linker conformational sampling
Blueprint file controls design
1000 trajectories per linker candidate

section_gists.heading: All-atom MD simulation
section_gists.gist: Initial models from SWISS-MODEL. GROMACS with AMBER99SB-ILDN force field, SPC water, 0.15 M NaCl. Energy minimization, NVT/NPT equilibration. 3x300 ns production runs per construct. PME for electrostatics, 1.4 nm cutoffs, LINCS constraints.
process_signals: Triplicate 300 ns simulations for statistical reliability
Standard MD protocol

section_gists.heading: Free energy landscape analysis
section_gists.gist: PCA on Cα coordinates to get PC1 and PC2. FELs constructed using probability distribution from MD. Also used RMSD and Rg as coordinates. Bio3D and R for analysis, Matplotlib for visualization.
process_signals: PCA to reduce dimensionality
FELs show conformational heterogeneity
RMSD/Rg FELs as complementary analysis

section_gists.heading: MM/GBSA calculation
section_gists.gist: gmx_MMPBSA used with GB^OBC1 model (igb=2). 1500 frames from last 150 ns. Binding free energy computed as difference between complex and monomers. Mean and SD from three independent simulations.
process_signals: MM/GBSA for relative ranking of self-assembly
1500 frames from equilibrated trajectories
failures_or_limits: MM/GBSA not accurate for absolute binding free energies, only ranking

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The research follows a linear design-build-test cycle: computational design of trivalent constructs, MD-based evaluation to select optimal candidate, experimental validation, and structural confirmation.
The initial hypothesis was that multivalent binding to all three RBDs would enhance avidity and neutralization breadth, which was confirmed by MP-5ff and Tr67.
The choice of linker length and type was critical: n=2 too short, n=4 irregular, n=3 and 5 suitable; flexible vs rigid linkers affected conformational homogeneity.
The F-scaffold (foldon) outperformed C-scaffold (coiled-coil) in trimerization efficiency and homogeneity.
The 5ff scaffold (F-scaffold with (GGGGS)5 linker) was the optimal design, generalizable to other microproteins like nanobodies.
Tr67's broad neutralization stems from synergistic binding that locks spike in 3-RBD-up, a rare conformation for Omicron, enhancing immune clearance potential.
Computational evaluation (MM/GBSA, FEL) successfully predicted experimental outcomes, validating the rational design approach.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Were any iterative design cycles performed after initial experimental results, or was the process strictly linear?
Why was n=4 linker rejected based on irregular distance distribution? Could it have been explored further?
What specific mutations in cluster 2 variants abrogate Nb67 binding? The paper mentions F486V but are there others?
How does the 9 Å cryo-EM resolution affect confidence in atomic model fitting?
Are there any off-target effects or cytotoxicity concerns with Tr67?
What is the stability and shelf-life of these microproteins?
Could the 5ff scaffold be used with other binders beyond miniACE2 and Nb67?

events.summary: Need for broad-spectrum antivirals against evolving SARS-CoV-2 variants that evade existing drugs and reduce conserved epitopes.
Multivalent drugs can synergistically bind to the three RBDs of the S protein, enhancing therapeutic effectiveness.

Computational approach developed to design self-assembling trivalent microproteins targeting the S protein, involving four steps: structure-guided linker design, molecular simulation evaluation, experimental validation, and functional testing.
Design goal to trap the active 3-RBD-up conformation by fully occupying all three RBDs with a trivalent protein.
Calculation of minimum linker distances: 19.37 Å for F-scaffold and 17.98 Å for C-scaffold using Lagrange multiplier method.
RosettaRemodel used to sample linker conformations for (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n with n=2-5; n=2 rejected as too short, n=4 less preferable due to irregular distance distribution, n=3 and 5 selected.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers introduction and structure-guided linker design section, establishing problem, hypothesis, and initial design steps.

events.summary: Linkers with n=2 rejected as unsuitable because most distances did not exceed minimum distances.
Linkers with n=4 rejected due to irregular distance distributions, making n=4 less preferable.
Four test linkers chosen: flexible (GGGGS)3 and (GGGGS)5, rigid (EAAAK)3 and (EAAAK)5.
MM/GBSA used to estimate binding free energies between monomers; F-scaffold constructs generally have lower binding free energies than C-scaffold constructs.
MP-5ff has the lowest relative binding free energy among F-scaffold constructs, implying strongest self-assembly tendency.
PCA and free energy landscapes (FELs) used to assess conformational homogeneity; MP-5ff shows a single narrow energy well indicating high homogeneity.
MM/GBSA has limitations in predicting absolute binding free energies but excels in ranking relative binding affinities.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers linker design decisions (rejection of n=2 and n=4, selection of n=3 and n=5), MD simulation setup, MM/GBSA ranking, and PCA/FEL analysis for conformational homogeneity. Key events include design choices and computational evaluation.

events.summary: FEL analysis shows MP-5ff has a single low-energy well, indicating best conformational homogeneity.
RMSD/Rg FELs confirm MP-5ff has a single stable trimer conformation, while others have multiple low-energy wells.
SEC shows F-scaffold proteins have sharper trimer peaks than C-scaffold; MP-5ff is the sharpest and most concentrated, indicating highest trimerization efficiency.
Native-PAGE shows only MP-5ff presents a single protein band, confirming a single stable trimer conformation.
The 5ff trimerization unit is applied to nanobody Nb67 to create trivalent nanobody Tr67.
Experimental results confirm computational predictions: MP-5ff has highest trimerization efficiency and conformational homogeneity.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers FEL analysis, experimental validation (SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, neutralization), and design of Tr67. Events focus on analysis and experimental confirmation of MP-5ff superiority and generalization to nanobody.

events.summary: SEC profiles of purified trivalent miniACE2 constructs show F-scaffold proteins have sharper trimer peaks than C-scaffold; MP-5ff sharpest, MP-5rf two peaks.
Native-PAGE analysis shows only MP-5ff has a single band, indicating high conformational homogeneity; other constructs show multiple bands.
BLI measurements show MP-5ff has KD <1 pM, a 1000-fold improvement over monovalent miniACE2 (KD 1.03 nM).
SEC and Native-PAGE confirm Tr67 has high trimerization efficiency and conformational homogeneity similar to MP-5ff.
BLI shows Tr67 has KD 0.746 nM, about 20-fold affinity increase over monovalent Nb67 (KD 15.2 nM) against Omicron BA.1 RBD.
Tr67 neutralizes Omicron BA.1 pseudovirus with IC50 55 pM, enhanced over Nb67 (IC50 492 pM).

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains experimental validation of MP-5ff and design/characterization of Tr67, including SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI, and neutralization data.

events.summary: Tr67 shows broad neutralization against Omicron variants, while monovalent Nb67 fails against cluster 2 variants.
numbers_or_metrics: IC50 values for Tr67: 0.5 nM (BA.5), 0.8 nM (BF.7), 1.2 nM (BQ.1.1), 14.6 nM (XBB.1), 14.6 nM (XBB.1.5)

events.summary: Cryo-EM confirms Tr67 binds all three RBDs in 3-RBD-up conformation, consistent with design.
Interface analysis shows Tr67 has larger contact area and more interactions than monovalent Nb67.
numbers_or_metrics: Tr67 interface: 21 residues from Nb67, 25 from RBD; monovalent Nb67: 14 and 16 residues

events.summary: Docking simulations explain why Nb67 fails against cluster 2 variants: mutations weaken interactions, Nb67 docks to wrong sites.
entities: cluster 2 variants (BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, XBB.1.5)

events.summary: Tr67's synergistic binding increases interface and resists mutations, enabling broad neutralization.
Further structural studies needed to elucidate detailed molecular mechanisms of Tr67's broad neutralization.
Computational approach enables precise design of multivalent proteins geometrically matching target binding sites.
Rational approach narrows design space and reduces randomness compared to experimental screening.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains experimental neutralization data, cryo-EM validation, interface analysis, docking simulations, and discussion of design approach advantages.

events.summary: Cryo-EM structure of Tr67 with Omicron BA.1 spike captured in 3-RBD-up conformation, providing mechanistic insights into antiviral activity.
Tr67 induces 3-RBD-up conformation by synergistically interacting with the same epitope on three RBDs, distinct from antibody combinations.
Tr67 not only blocks ACE2 binding but also leads spike to a conformational state more susceptible to immune clearance.
Multivalent binding results in avidity, wider binding interfaces, and ensures all three binders remain tightly associated with RBDs.
Study focused on computational design and in vitro validation; in vivo therapeutic efficacy, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and biosafety not evaluated.
RosettaRemodel used to design linkers connecting miniACE2 and trimerization scaffold, testing (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n with n=2-5.
All-atom MD simulations performed using GROMACS with AMBER99SB-ILDN force field, three independent 300-ns runs per construct.
MM/GBSA binding free energy calculated using gmx_MMPBSA with GB^OBC1 model, 1500 frames from last 150 ns.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk primarily contains detailed methods for pseudovirus neutralization assay and cryo-EM data collection/processing. It provides experimental protocols and computational steps but no new design or evaluation events beyond what was already described in earlier sections. The events extracted are procedural and confirm the experimental validation of Tr67.
This chunk contains only reference citations and a publisher's note, with no research process events.

evidence_bank.quote: the ongoing mutations in SARS-CoV-2 not only diminish the efficacy of existing drugs, but also reduce the number of conserved epitopes in drug targets, posing a significant challenge to drug design and development
there is an urgent need to develop novel therapeutic agents that can effectively combat emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants, such as broad-spectrum neutralizing antibodies or other protein drugs

multivalent drugs can use their pharmacophores (e.g., an antibody to a single RBD) to synergistically bind to the three RBDs of the S protein, thereby enhancing therapeutic effectiveness against the viruses

we developed a computational approach to engineer self-assembling trivalent microproteins that simultaneously bind to the three receptor binding domains (RBDs) of the S protein. This approach involves four steps: structure-guided linker design, molecular simulation evaluation of self-assembly, experimental validation of self-assembly state, and functional testing.
our designed goal was to trap the active RBD-up conformation by fully occupying all three RBDs with a designed trivalent protein

we calculated the minimum distance required for a linker to connect miniACE2 and the given trimerization scaffold using the Lagrange multiplier method, and found that the minimum distances for the F- and C-scaffolds are 19.37 Å and 17.98 Å, respectively

the distance distributions for n=4 were irregular, neither as narrow as n=3 nor as broad as n=5; furthermore, those for n=3 and 5 have covered most of the range seen in n=4, making n=4 less preferable
indicating that linkers designed with n=2 may not be suitable

the distance distributions for n=4 were irregular, neither as narrow as n=3 nor as broad as n=5; furthermore, those for n=3 and 5 have covered most of the range seen in n=4, making n=4 less preferable
we chose four test linkers, the flexible $(GGGGS)_{3}$ and $(GGGGS)_{5}$ , and the rigid $(EAAAK)_{3}$ and $(EAAAK)_{5}$

except MP-5ff the FELs of all constructs had multiple low-energy wells (in blue), suggesting that multiple low-energy trimer-like conformations coexist in the simulations.
the peak of MP-5ff appears to be the sharpest and the most concentrated one, indicating that it is the most efficient trivalent construct
only MP-5ff presented a single protein band, indicating a single stable trimer conformation
we applied the 5ff trimerization unit to engineer a trivalent nanobody targeting the dominant circulating Omicron variants
MP-5ff was found to be the best construct with the highest trimerization efficiency and conformational homogeneity.
SEC profiles of the purified proteins of the constructs.
Native-PAGE analysis of the protein trimer fractions isolated from SEC.

Tr67 has a trimerization efficiency and conformational homogeneity very similar to that of MP-5ff.

Tr67 achieved this by synergistically interacting with the same epitope on the three RBDs. In this sense, the induced mechanism of the 3-RBD-up conformation by Tr67 is different from the previous one.
by inducing the 3-RBD-up conformation, Tr67 not only blocks the ACE2 binding to any RBD of the S protein, but also leads the S protein to a conformational state more susceptible to immune clearance.
This not only results in wider binding interfaces (e.g., in Fig. 8e), but also ensures that all three bound binders (Nb67s) in Tr67 remain tightly associated with the RBDs.

RosettaRemodel [62] was used to design linkers connecting the C-terminus of miniACE2 and the N-terminus of the trimerization scaffold.
We tested $(GGGGS)_{n}$ and $(EAAAK)_{n}$ linkers with n=2, 3, 4, and 5, respectively
For each linker candidate, 1000 independent trajectories were sampled
All-atom MD simulations were performed using GROMACS software with AMBER 99SB-ILDN force field
For each construct, three independent 300-ns production runs were performed.
1500 frames were extracted from the last 150 ns of each trajectory for analysis.
infection was quantified by luminescence measurement using a luminescence meter (PerkinElmer, Cat. No. HH34000000).
All cryo-EM data were collected on a 200 kV FEI Glacios Cryo Transmission Electron Microscope (Thermo Fisher Scientific) equipped with a FEI Falcon 3EC direct detector.
Movie stacks were collected automatically using the EPU software (Thermo Fisher Scientific) at a magnification of 92,000× with a pixel size of 1.57 Å.
The defocus range was set from – 1.8 to – 2.4 μm.
In total, 4969 videos were acquired for the single particle analysis.
Contrast transfer function (CTF) value estimations were performed by Patch CTF Estimation.
Particle coordinates were automatically picked by PARSED [80] with diameters from 100 to 300 Å, and the 5,250,976 particles were extracted with a box size of 280 pixels and a pixel size of 1.57 Å.
Several rounds of iterative 2D classifications were then performed to select well-defined particle images, and about 254,339 particles were selected for further 3D reconstruction.
The cryo-EM density map of Tr67 in complex with the spike protein of Omicron BA.1 has been deposited in the Electron Microscopy Data Bank with accession code EMD-36850.

Also, the “1-RBD-up, 2-RBD-down” conformation was considered to give the Omicron variants an advantage in evading the immune system, because many antibodies fail to bind their epitopes when two of the RBDs are in the “down” state $[38, 45]$ .

It appears that the synergistic binding of the three Nb67s in Tr67 increases the binding interface and then leads to higher binding affinities that could resist the mutations such as that at 486 to some extent.
Particularly, similar to a previous study $[35]$ , we found that the mutation at 486 plays a key role in this process (Additional file 1: Fig.
Unlike the spikes of early variants such as Wuhan-Hu-1, the Omicron spike rarely occurs in the 3-RBD-up conformation, which may need to be induced by a combination of distinct antibodies $[39, 40]$ .

Due to its amino acid mutations and RBD-RBD interactions, the Omicron spike is usually stabilized in the “2-RBD-down, 1-RBD-up” conformation; this conformational state was considered to facilitate the up-RBD to approach ACE2 and then to promote membrane fusion $[36–38]$ .

It has been reported that 85% of the 247 antibodies that directly target the S protein fail to neutralize the Omicron variants $[6]$ .

Thus, compared with its monovalent counterpart, Tr67 has a significant increase in the neutralizing activity against all the tested Omicron variants, suggesting that multivalent proteins have the potential to be developed into broad-spectrum drugs against the emerging SARS-CoV-2 variants.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Need for broad-spectrum antivirals against evolving SARS-CoV-2 variants that evade existing monovalent drugs.

canonical_events.summary: SARS-CoV-2 mutations reduce efficacy of existing drugs and limit conserved epitopes, creating urgent need for broad-spectrum antivirals.
Multivalent drugs can synergistically bind all three RBDs of the S protein, enhancing avidity and neutralization breadth.
Computational approach developed to design self-assembling trivalent microproteins targeting the S protein, with goal to trap 3-RBD-up conformation.
Selected miniACE2 as monovalent binder and two trimerization scaffolds: F-scaffold (T4 fibritin foldon) and C-scaffold (coiled-coil).
Calculated minimum linker distances: 19.37 Å for F-scaffold, 17.98 Å for C-scaffold.
RosettaRemodel sampled linker conformations for (GGGGS)n and (EAAAK)n with n=2-5; n=2 rejected as too short, n=4 rejected due to irregular distance distributions, n=3 and 5 selected.

MD simulations (3x300 ns) and MM/GBSA binding free energy calculations predicted self-assembly: F-scaffold constructs generally more stable; MP-5ff had lowest relative binding free energy among F-scaffolds.
PCA-based free energy landscapes (FELs) showed MP-5ff has a single narrow energy well, indicating high conformational homogeneity; other constructs showed multiple wells.

Experimental validation: SEC and Native-PAGE confirmed MP-5ff has highest trimerization efficiency and single stable conformation; BLI showed KD <1 pM (1000-fold improvement over monovalent miniACE2); pseudovirus neutralization IC50 29 pM (23-fold improvement).
Applied 5ff trimerization unit to nanobody Nb67 to create trivalent nanobody Tr67.
Tr67 showed high trimerization efficiency and homogeneity (SEC, Native-PAGE); BLI KD 0.746 nM (20-fold over Nb67); neutralized Omicron BA.1 with IC50 55 pM.
Tr67 broadly neutralized Omicron variants including cluster 2 (BA.5, BF.7, BQ.1.1, XBB.1, XBB.1.5), while monovalent Nb67 failed against cluster 2.
Cryo-EM structure of Tr67 with Omicron BA.1 spike at 9 Å resolution confirmed Tr67 binds all three RBDs in 3-RBD-up conformation, locking them in inactive state.

Interface analysis and docking simulations explained broad neutralization: Tr67 has larger contact area and more interactions; Nb67 fails to dock to cluster 2 variants at expected epitope due to mutations.
Study limited to computational design and in vitro validation; in vivo therapeutic efficacy, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and biosafety not evaluated.

edges.reason: Design approach requires selection of components.
Component selection enables linker distance calculation.
Linker design yields constructs for MD evaluation.
MD simulations provide data for FEL analysis.
Success of MP-5ff motivates generalization to nanobody.
Tr67 design leads to experimental characterization.
Initial neutralization data leads to broad variant testing.
Cryo-EM structure enables interface and docking analysis.

must_cover_points: Problem of SARS-CoV-2 immune evasion and need for broad-spectrum antivirals.
Hypothesis that multivalent binding enhances avidity and breadth.
Computational design approach with structure-guided linker design.
Linker length optimization (rejection of n=2, n=4; selection of n=3,5).
MD simulations and MM/GBSA ranking identifying MP-5ff as optimal.
FEL analysis showing MP-5ff conformational homogeneity.
Experimental validation of MP-5ff: SEC, Native-PAGE, BLI (KD <1 pM), neutralization (IC50 29 pM).
Tr67 broad neutralization against Omicron variants, including cluster 2.
Interface and docking analysis explaining broad neutralization.
